invalidation.condition: Any 2 of the 3 largest hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google, Amazon) cut AI capex guidance by >25% vs the prior quarter in the same earnings season.
invalidation.eval_note: NOT machine-checkable via yfinance — requires reading hyperscaler earnings guidance. exit_watcher routes this to a Claude-checks-with-WebSearch item. This is the structural-deactivation stop: a real, broad capex cut (not the 2026-06-05 'peak scare', which was a missed UPGRADE, not a cut) breaks the thesis that funds the whole AI complex.
invalidation.rationale: The entire position rests on the AI-capex supercycle. A simultaneous >25% guidance cut at two of the three mega-buyers is the cleanest objective signal that demand is contracting, not just decelerating. Full exit, not reduce — if this fires, the thesis is dead.

invalidation.condition: SEMI.L closes more than 20% below entry price for 10 consecutive trading days.
invalidation.eval_note: Machine-checkable: SEMI.L is the traded vehicle and yfinance-resolvable. threshold 12.66 = provisional entry 15.83 - 20%; equals stop_price_level. NOTE: entry price/qty are PROVISIONAL (yfinance quoted 15.83 GBP; the etf_universe lists a USD line) — reconcile both the price and the currency against the actual Revolut fill and re-derive the threshold then.
invalidation.rationale: A 20% drawdown held for 10 sessions triggers a REVIEW, not a reflex exit. Distinguish a market-wide risk-off (hold/add if fundamentals intact) from a sector-specific de-rating. Only exit if paired with a fired fundamental stop (inv_01/inv_03).

invalidation.condition: US AI-chip export controls (cat_20260601) escalate to a broad ban that cuts >15% of the leading-edge design names' addressable revenue (e.g. a guided China-revenue reduction at NVDA/AMD/Broadcom or a TSMC leading-edge order cut attributable to controls).
invalidation.eval_note: NOT machine-checkable — requires reading export-control policy + company revenue guidance. Claude-checks-with-WebSearch. This is the SECONDARY catalyst on the sector (an active RISK, not a buy-driver) materializing into a real revenue hit.
invalidation.rationale: Export controls are the asymmetric regulatory tail for this sub-sector specifically. A narrow control is priced; a broad ban that removes >15% of TAM compresses the multiple AND the earnings base. Reduce to a satellite size and reassess on the ex-China growth alone.

assumptions.statement: Combined hyperscaler AI capex guidance (Microsoft + Google + Amazon + Meta) stays above $100B/quarter during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: quarterly earnings transcripts / 10-Q. Falsified by: any quarter where combined guidance falls below $100B AND is a >30% sequential cut.
assumptions.status_note: Q1 2026: ~$700B annual = ~$175B/quarter; all four raised guidance. The 2026-06-05 Broadcom 'peak scare' was a missed UPGRADE, not a cut — threshold not at risk.

assumptions.statement: Leading-edge design demand (NVDA/AVGO/AMD/TSMC AI revenue) keeps growing YoY — the AI compute build-out is not yet at peak units.
assumptions.measurability: Source: company AI/datacenter segment revenue (quarterly). Falsified by: sector AI-revenue YoY turns negative for 2 consecutive quarters.
assumptions.status_note: Demand still accelerating as of Q1 2026; the open question the experiment tests is whether the RATE is peaking.

assumptions.statement: US AI-chip export controls (cat_20260601) stay narrow — they do not broaden to cut >15% of the sector's addressable revenue.
assumptions.measurability: Source: BIS/Commerce policy + company China-revenue guidance. Falsified by: a guided China-revenue cut >15% attributable to new controls.
assumptions.status_note: cat_20260601 active, priced_in 0.25. Contained as of entry; this is the live regulatory risk to watch.

assumptions.statement: The AI-semiconductor narrative does not flip from 'crowded' to 'exhausted' (positioning unwind) during the horizon.
assumptions.measurability: Source: narrative_maturity classification + flows. Falsified by: narrative_maturity reaches 'exhausted' OR two consecutive months of sector ETF net outflows.
assumptions.status_note: narrative_maturity = 'crowded' at entry (acknowledged: less alpha left). Regime = 'contested' (AI-capex peak scare, classified noise). Both are explicit, accepted risks of this experiment.

risk_discipline.note: ⚠ CORRELATED-CATALYST CAP OVERRIDE (authorized by user, 2026-06-08). This move pushes DIRECT struct_ai_capex_supercycle exposure to ~€650/€2000 = 32.5%, over the 20% max_combined_pct cap (enforcement: warn). Counting copper's PARENT theme (struct_copper_datacenter_demand is the copper expression of AI capex), the true AI-cluster concentration is ~€1650/€2000 ≈ 82%. This concentration is NOT an oversight — it is the EXPERIMENT'S EXPLICIT VARIABLE: a deliberate, sized, monitored test of the AI-capex supercycle via a third (design) expression, on top of the copper (commodity) and grid (power) expressions already held. Mitigants: (1) small conviction / €500 scale-in first tranche; (2) timing was the only green light — neutral/enter_now after a -2.9%/5d cooldown from overbought; (3) hard full-exit stop on a real capex cut (inv_01). Decision Journal experiment #1.

metadata.attribution_note: Single driver: struct_ai_capex_supercycle at 1.0. The sector study also lists cat_20260601_us_ai_chip_export_controls, but that event is a RISK (captured in inv_03/asm_03), not a positive buy-driver, so it carries no attribution weight.
metadata.fill_note: amount_eur=500 is AUTHORITATIVE. qty 27.33 is USER-CONFIRMED (Revolut, 2026-06-08, iShares MSCI Global Semiconductors UCITS). price 15.83 is the native GBP close per yfinance for SEMI.L; 27.33 × 15.83 GBP ≈ £433 ≈ €500 at ~1.156 GBP/EUR (EUR-effective ≈ €18.30/unit). nav_engine values via SEMI.L (GBP) × qty with FX conversion. NOTE: the etf_universe lists this line as USD — verify the SEMI.L quote currency at quarterly review and re-derive inv_02's 12.66 threshold if it changes.

experiment.hypothesis: The AI-capex supercycle remains the dominant return driver and the design sub-sector (SEMI.L) captures it with positive forward return over the horizon, justifying a third correlated expression on top of copper (commodity) and grid (power).
experiment.what_would_disprove: inv_01 fires (real broad capex cut) OR the design names' AI revenue turns negative YoY (asm_02) — i.e. the supercycle decelerates to contraction. A market-wide risk-off drawdown alone does NOT disprove it.
accepted_risks: AI-capex concentration far over the 20% correlated cap (deliberate variable)
USD/GBP FX + currency ambiguity on the vehicle

entry_technicals.source: technical_study.py (deep TA, opt-in via catalyx-open step 5.6)
bearish: MACD bearish cross — histogram just turned negative (short-term momentum flattening)
cautions: 87.6% of 52w range — buying near the highs after a big run (no overhead resistance = price discovery, cuts both ways)

sizing_decision.ta_recommendation: scale_in — first tranche ~€250-300, planned add on a pullback toward the 13.58 swing support (~-12%)
sizing_decision.rationale: Deliberate override of the scale-in suggestion: the position is satellite-sized (€500), the fundamental backdrop is strong (AI-capex intact, hyperscaler capex ~$700B), and entry_timing was green (neutral/enter_now after a -2.9%/5d cooldown). The TA cautions (extended vs 200d, MACD rollover) are acknowledged and bounded by inv_02 (the -20%/10d review stop at 12.66) — not large enough to fractionalize a €500 experiment. Documented here so the close can judge whether full-size-now was the right call vs the scale-in the TA preferred.
